question: Can ROCm, Rust, and HIP engines materially speed up the current G1 residual-gate push?

host_ilu_device_gmres_coupled_current.assessment: Fast enough for coupled sparse linear correction when the matrix/preconditioner combination is favorable.

host_ilu_device_gmres_shell_current.assessment: Converges but is not a fast path for shell-dominant residual work because Krylov iteration count saturates.

g1_newton_host_ilu_device_gmres_1iter_at.assessment: Directly swapping the current G1 Newton profile to host_ilu_device_gmres is not a quick residual-gate path.

hipsparse_ilu0_focused_existing.assessment: Prebuilt hipSPARSE ILU0-BiCGStab is unstable for the shell system and should not be promoted for G1.

ginkgo_hip_existing.assessment: Not currently available as a G1 acceleration dependency.

rust_path.assessment: Rust backend is useful for existing runtime/NDTHA/nonlinear-frame contracts, but it is not wired into the current MGT full 3D frame+shell residual/Jacobian assembly.

shell_bending_tangent_followup.assessment: Same-component shell-bending tangent correction is now saturated; it still descends, but only at micro-Newton scale.

translation_wide_tangent_followup.assessment: Widening to mixed translation rows does not create descent from the current frontier.

latest_hotspot_direction_counter_evidenc.assessment: The newest local tangent probes show that balanced component+DOF row selection improves over raw top-row selection, but residual descent remains micro-scale. The active blocker is still correction-direction quality, not raw evaluation throughput.

global_direction_jvp.assessment: Current assembled tangent action is finite-difference consistent for global diagnostic directions, so raw tangent mismatch is not the active G1 blocker.

shell_bending_hotspot_tangent_fd_jvp.assessment: The dominant shell-bending hotspot rows are also tangent/FD consistent; the remaining 0.28038 N residual is not explained by a shell-bending Jacobian sign or scale error.

low_regularization_global_newton.assessment: Lowering the direct regularization floor did not produce descent; all positive line-search trials remained above the current frontier.

signed_low_regularization_global_newton.assessment: Allowing negative alphas still did not find descent, so a faster sparse solve alone is unlikely to close the G1 residual gate without a better correction model.

current_frontier_jvp_and_lowreg_counter_.assessment: ROCm/HIP can reduce selected sparse solve and diagnostic wall time, but the latest component+DOF node-block evidence keeps the conclusion unchanged: the current frontier is dominated by correction model and conditioning limits rather than by backend throughput alone.

row_column_wide16_support128_polish.note: Requested max_rows=16/support128, but the pre-existing top-row list only exposed eight shell-bending rows before component_top_count was added.

row_column_top64_wide16_support128_no_de.assessment: After component_top_count exposed 16 shell-bending rows, the unregularized row-column LSTSQ direction became physically unusable; even tiny alphas increased residual.

row_column_top64_wide16_support128_ridge.assessment: Ridge plus near-null column scale-floor protection converted the same top64/16-row support from no-descent correction blow-up into a small accepted polishing step.
At the coarse alpha grid, ridge factors 1e-1 and 1e-3 gave the same accepted residual; line-search resolution dominated this local polishing step.

mixed_translation_top64_rows32_support12.assessment: A wider mixed local block direction did not reduce the physical residual at the latest frontier.

shell_membrane_top64_rows16_support128_r.assessment: Shell-membrane-only local correction fits the selected linear rows but still does not move the global physical residual.

frame_top64_rows10_support128_ridge1em3_.assessment: Frame-only local correction also produces no residual descent at the latest frontier.

all_dof_nodeblock_top64_rows32_support8_.assessment: Allowing all DOF rows and expanding support to structural node blocks reopened a coupled descent path after translation-only/component-specific probes saturated.
The same all-DOF node-block branch saturated after two promotions; the next raw direction became too large and residual-worsening.
Increasing ridge to 0.1 at the saturated frontier did not recover descent.
Very strong ridge reduced the correction size but still left the frontier flat; ridge magnitude alone is not the next lever.
Tiny alpha sweep confirmed the followup2 node-block frontier is locally flat until larger alphas worsen residual.

conditioned_block_lstsq_frontier_evidenc.assessment: Conditioning-aware block LSTSQ is a real but saturating descent family. Narrow shell-bending ridge polish reached 0.2803796687 N, while translation-only, shell-membrane-only, and frame-only probes produced no descent. Expanding target rows to all DOFs and support to structural node blocks reopened coupled descent to 0.2803795738 N, then saturated when the raw correction grew to millimeter scale. Ridge factors 0.1 and 10.0 did not recover descent at the saturated frontier, so the next G1 push should adapt support/target selection or move to a broader coarse/global correction rather than simply increasing damping.

finite_difference_residual_only_counter_.assessment: Residual-only finite-difference evaluation is cheap enough after shell operator caching, but local hotspot least-squares corrections do not create descent at the current frontier.

decision.recommended_g1_path: Do not spend the next G1 push on raw engine acceleration alone. Keep the cached residual-only path, promote the new all-DOF node-block correction into an adaptive ridge/support/trust loop, and use ROCm/HIP selectively for repeated sparse/JVP diagnostics and broader coarse/global correction experiments.
why: The current residual decay is limited by correction direction quality, not only by per-evaluation speed.
The latest shell-bending tangent follow-up improved only 6.98e-7 N, and the wider translation tangent probe produced no descent from 0.28038028917462354 N.
Current global and shell-bending hotspot JVP checks are finite-difference consistent at roughly 1e-10 relative error, so the active blocker is not a simple tangent sign/scale mismatch.
A low-regularization direct Newton attempt, including signed alphas, found no residual descent from the current frontier.
Conditioned block LSTSQ produced accepted descent to 0.2803796687027861 N, but the improvement decayed from 5.54e-7 N to 1.41e-9 N across follow-ups.
After exposing top64 residual rows, the 16-row shell-bending LSTSQ correction was too ill-conditioned to descend even under tiny alpha search.
Ridge regularization plus near-null column scale-floor protection converted the top64/16-row path into a controlled polishing step instead of a 5530 m correction blow-up.
Fine alpha search improved the ridge-polish branch, but the branch is now at nanonewton-scale descent and not a credible path to the 1e-3 N gate by itself.

The latest top64 component probe shows a mixed frontier, with 29 shell-bending rows, 25 shell-membrane rows, and 10 frame rows; the next correction needs coupled shell-membrane/frame participation rather than another narrow shell-bending polish.
At the latest 0.2803796687027861 N frontier, mixed translation, shell-membrane-only, and frame-only ridge probes all ended with no gate-eligible descent.
The all-DOF node-block ridge branch reopened coupled descent and advanced the frontier to 0.28037957384916096 N with two accepted promotions before saturating.
The new all-DOF node-block component probe shows the frontier shifted to 35 shell-bending rows, 22 shell-membrane rows, and 7 frame rows in the top64 residual set.
ROCm host-ILU/device-GMRES is fast on the favorable coupled sparse matrix but slow on shell-dominant systems and too slow when directly inserted into the current G1 Newton profile.
Rust/HIP runtime assets are not connected to the current MGT full 3D residual/Jacobian assembly.
Finite-difference residual-only local corrections can be evaluated without full assemblies, but their best candidates do not reduce the frontier residual.

balanced_block_lstsq_frontier_evidence.component_probe_frontier_note: The component probe was captured at the support1 frontier. The subsequent rows12/support2 and rows16/support3 followups only moved the residual by 2.3283064365386963e-09 N total, so the dominant component distribution is treated as unchanged for acceleration triage.
balanced_block_lstsq_frontier_evidence.assessment: Component+DOF round-robin target selection with node-block support reopens a better local descent path than raw top-row selection. The strongest pass in this batch used rows16/support2 and improved by 8.7195e-08 N, but later followups collapsed to 1e-09 N scale. Rows24, support1, rows12/support2, and rows16/support3 all show that simple target widening or support retuning is not enough to close the 1e-3 N gate quickly.

cached_residual_jvp_batch_frontier_evide.assessment: Cached residual-only FD-JVP remains useful as an inexpensive diagnostic and produced one tiny frontier promotion, but alpha-wide replay, rows8/support24 broadening, and pure top-residual rows4/support24 all failed to improve beyond 0.28037931296242474 N. The bottleneck is now correction-direction/model consistency, not the small LSTSQ solve or alpha sweep. The next G1 closure attempt should move to a consistent/global residual-Jacobian or material/full-load Newton lane rather than repeating local cached FD-JVP polishing.

alpha_wide_replay_from_first_pass.assessment: Wide alpha replay did not beat the existing cached rows4/support12 frontier; larger alpha values quickly worsen the direct residual.

broadened_rows8_support24_alpha_wide_rep.large_alpha_observation: alpha >= 1e-5 increased the direct residual; alpha around 3e-6 or smaller was numerically neutral.

top_rows4_support24_alpha_wide.assessment: Pure top-residual shell-bending rows worsened the direct residual for every tested alpha, including signed alphas.

current_acceleration_decision.rocm_hip_decision: Residual-only matvec/trial routing is the current G1 acceleration win: it avoids repeated full tangent assembly during global Krylov matvecs and line-search trials while preserving direct physical residual replay. This CPU path promoted the frontier twice before any HIP port, so ROCm/HIP work should now target batched residual-only JVP matvecs and sparse preconditioner solves for this global operator, not local LSTSQ polishing.
current_acceleration_decision.rust_decision: The Rust/HIP runtime path remains useful for existing nonlinear-frame/NDTHA contracts, but it is not connected to the current MGT full 3D frame+shell residual/Jacobian assembly. Wiring it now would be integration work before algorithmic closure evidence, not a quick gate-closing path.
current_acceleration_decision.next_g1_step: Latest frontier hotspot JVP consistency is already strong (max relative inf error below 1e-9 on shell_bending_drilling uz hotspots), while restart-depth and input-scale sweeps did not descend. The next G1 work should target shell bending/full-load/material Newton state and continuation rather than more local/global line-search parameter polishing. The residual gate remains open by about 0.279379 N.

global_krylov_residual_only_frontier_evi.assessment: Residual-only finite-difference matvecs and residual-only alpha trials moved the active G1 frontier twice: 0.28037931296242474 -> 0.28037924622609695 -> 0.28037907020613034 N. Every recorded global Krylov run used 2/2 residual-only matvecs and 30/30 residual-only trials with zero full-assembly trial/matvec calls. At the new frontier, repeat reg1e-5 and reg1e-4 did not descend; reg1e-6 and reg1e-7 also failed from the intermediate frontier. The next productive branch is a controlled sweep over Krylov restart depth/preconditioner input scale or a multi-step adaptive controller, not another identical single-restart run.

input_scale_counter_sweep.assessment: At the current reg1e-5 residual-only global Krylov frontier, preconditioner input scales 0.3, 3.0, and 1.0 all replayed with residual-only matvec/trial evidence but did not produce residual descent.

restart2_counter_sweep.assessment: At the current 0.28037907020613034 N frontier, Krylov max_iterations/restart depth 2 did not improve for reg1e-5 or reg1e-6. Both runs used residual-only matvecs/trials only, so the negative result is directional/model evidence rather than full tangent assembly overhead.

latest_frontier_residual_jacobian_consis.assessment: At the latest residual-only global Krylov frontier, tangent-vs-finite-difference JVP consistency is strong on the dominant hotspots. The top residual field is shell dominated, and the first 8 hotspot JVP rows are all shell_bending_drilling uz DOFs with max relative inf error below 1e-9. This makes repeated line-search/preconditioner polishing less attractive; the next closure work should address shell bending/full-load/material state modeling or continuation, not basic tangent/FD mismatch.

reference_shell_surface_load_reconstruct.assessment: The displayed top-row external loads reconstruct from the current reference triangular shell surface load rule to roundoff. The G1 hotspot is therefore not an external-load split or scalar load-scale wiring error.

latest_shell_load_projection_diagnosis.assessment: The current tangent local support cannot represent the dominant shell residual rows: the projection residual is effectively the same size as the target residual. The leading shell rows also show row-scale shell internal/reference-load patterns around 0.25 and -0.5, while the shell surface load reconstruction matches the displayed external loads to roundoff. This points at shell internal replay/full-load transfer state before raw engine acceleration.

shell_patch_neighbor1_counterprobe.assessment: Expanding the top shell target to a one-hop shell patch with node-block support did not recover descent. The best residual-only candidate worsened the frontier, so the remaining G1 path should move away from local/patch row LSTSQ polishing toward full shell internal force transfer or global load-path correction.

checkpoint_preconditioned_global_counter.assessment: A minimal checkpoint-start global diagonal/node-block Jacobi counterprobe found no residual descent. The only best alpha was 0.0; the tested negative alpha worsened the residual, especially for node-block Jacobi. This weakens the case for a quick ROCm/HIP solver swap and strengthens the case for fixing the physical shell load-path/internal-force transfer before batching more solves.

shell_internal_element_load_path_diagnos.assessment: The dominant shell rows now reconstruct from incident element stiffness contributions to within 3e-6 N. The top 8 uz rows belong to two one-element/four-node shell components with no frame-connected nodes and no restrained translational DOFs, yet each component carries a nonzero pressure resultant of 1.121516624 N. This explains why local row correction, patch correction, generic Jacobi, and faster sparse solves do not close the residual: the active frontier is a shell load-path attachment/free-pressure-component issue.

attached_pressure_policy_counterfactual.assessment: A diagnostic replay that suppresses pressure on surface components with no frame/support attachment lowers the current checkpoint residual from 0.280378 N to 0.115243 N without changing shell stiffness. The remaining active residual moves to the large attached shell component, so the next G1 work should combine this load-path policy with state cleanup/relaxation of the two suppressed free components and then resume correction on the attached shell component.

attached_policy_correction_lane.assessment: The diagnostic pressure policy has now been promoted into executable direct-residual correction evidence. Cleaning the two suppressed free shell components removed their stale displacement state, finite-difference residual-row correction on the large attached shell component reduced the frontier from 0.115243 N to 0.052893 N, largest-row rotation-focused corrections advanced it to 0.044372 N, and coupled node-block plus membrane/frame-normal corrections advanced it to 0.042156 N. The latest active row moved from node 2203 rx to node 2381 uz, dominated by shell membrane/frame cancellation; a frame-element coupled counterprobe produced only 7.76e-7 N additional descent. The gate is still not closed; the next correction needs a broader coupled attached-component membrane/frame equilibrium strategy rather than raw ROCm/HIP throughput alone.

decision.recommended_next_step: Continue from the attached-policy correction checkpoint and replace narrow local row polishing with a coupled attached-component shell bending/membrane/frame correction. ROCm/HIP should be reserved for batching the now-correct residual/JVP replays or sparse preconditioner solves after this coupled direction is defined.
why: ROCm runtime sees the RX 6900 XT, but the existing full G1 Newton swap to host-ILU/device-GMRES timed out beyond the 240 second budget.
Existing shell sparse GPU solve evidence converges but is slow for shell-dominant systems because Krylov iterations saturate.
Rust nonlinear-frame and NDTHA contracts are not wired to the current MGT full 3D frame-shell residual/Jacobian assembly.
The latest local tangent projection diagnostic shows almost no representable descent direction for the dominant shell residual rows.
The shell surface load reconstruction matches the displayed external load at roundoff, so the hotspot is not a simple external-load split bug.
A one-hop shell patch row-correction counterprobe also produced no residual descent.
A checkpoint-start global diagonal/node-block Jacobi counterprobe also produced no residual descent; the tested nonzero alpha worsened the current frontier.
The top 8 shell hotspot rows are attached to one-element free pressure components with no frame-connected nodes and no restrained translational DOFs; each carries a nonzero pressure resultant.

Suppressing pressure only on those unattached surface components lowers the current checkpoint residual by about 59%, from 0.280378 N to 0.115243 N, while leaving the large attached shell component loaded.

Promoting the policy into the direct-residual correction lane, cleaning the two suppressed free shell component states, and applying finite-difference residual-row correction reduced the frontier further to 0.052893 N.
Largest-row rotation-focused finite-difference correction advanced the frontier to 0.044372 N before the same narrow lane saturated with no residual descent.

Coupled residual-node-block corrections, a membrane-normal row correction, and a frame-coupled counterprobe advanced the frontier further to 0.042156 N with the latest frame-coupled improvement down to 7.76e-7 N.

The top active row is now an attached shell membrane uz residual at raw node 2381 with a large frame/shell membrane cancellation, while the top96 set remains mixed shell membrane, shell bending, and frame.
Current residual-only routing is already the practical acceleration win; the faster route to the gate is a better physical correction model, then GPU batching.
